edges.description: Reduced ACADVL activity blocks initial dehydrogenation of C12-C20 acyl-CoAs.
Biallelic ACADVL dysfunction is reflected by reduced measured VLCAD enzyme activity.

edges.target: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Impaired hepatic long-chain FAO limits ketogenesis, producing the hypoketotic biochemical state.
Blocked long-chain beta-oxidation produces elevated C14:1 acylcarnitine, the principal diagnostic marker.
Impaired long-chain acyl-CoA oxidation leads to abnormal long-chain acylcarnitine profiles.
FAO impairment is associated with reduced fasting glucagon concentrations in VLCAD patients.
Energy failure and impaired hepatic ketogenesis produce hypoketotic hypoglycemia during fasting or catabolic stress.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Myocardial energy deficit contributes to hypertrophic or dilated cardiomyopathy in severe VLCAD.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Skeletal muscle energy deficit during exercise or catabolic stress predisposes to rhabdomyolysis.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Hepatic energy deficit and impaired fatty acid handling contribute to hepatomegaly in hepatic VLCAD presentations.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Reduced skeletal muscle energy production causes exercise intolerance in the myopathic form.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Skeletal muscle energy failure manifests as muscle cramps and pain during myopathic episodes.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Recurrent skeletal muscle energy stress can be associated with persistent weakness.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Acute substrate stress can precipitate lethargic metabolic decompensation.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Severe neonatal metabolic decompensation may include secondary hyperammonemia.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Severe metabolic decompensation may include lactic acidosis in early-childhood VLCAD presentations.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Systemic and skeletal-muscle energy deficit contributes to hypotonia in severe early-onset disease.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Severe neurologic presentations in VLCAD can include status epilepticus and epileptic spasms.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Rhabdomyolysis from skeletal muscle energy failure raises serum creatine kinase.

edges.description: Vomiting can accompany severe infantile metabolic decompensation.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.target: Secondary mitochondrial dysfunction and OXPHOS impairment

edges.description: Long-chain acylcarnitine accumulation is linked to arrhythmogenic cardiac involvement.
Lipotoxic substrate handling abnormalities include excess long-chain carboxylic and dicarboxylic acid intermediates.

edges.description: Oxidative stress in VLCAD deficiency is reflected by glutathione pathway vulnerability and altered glutathione levels.

edges.description: Elevated C14:1 acylcarnitine reports the blocked long-chain FAO step in VLCAD deficiency.
Higher newborn-screening C14:1 predicts lower residual VLCAD enzyme activity, linking the biomarker to molecular-function deficiency.
Elevated long-chain acylcarnitine species report accumulation of incompletely oxidized long-chain FAO intermediates.
Elevated urinary long-chain carboxylic and dicarboxylic acids report abnormal long-chain substrate handling during VLCAD decompensation.
Inappropriately low ketone bodies report impaired hepatic ketogenesis downstream of the long-chain FAO block.
Low ketone production is the biochemical readout of the hypoketotic component of VLCAD-associated hypoglycemia.
Lower fasting glucagon is associated with VLCAD-related FAO impairment and may reflect reduced counter-regulatory support during fasting.
edges.source: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

edges.description: Glutathione is best interpreted as a stress-context-dependent readout: VLCAD-deficient cells can deplete GSH under metabolic stress even when broader metabolomic profiles suggest oxidative-stress remodeling.
Reduced measured VLCAD enzyme activity reports loss of ACADVL molecular function and helps stratify residual activity.

nodes.description: Biallelic ACADVL pathogenic variants reduce very long-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase catalytic activity.

evidence.reference_title: The Pathogenesis of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Very long-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (VLCADD) is a fatty acid β-oxidation disorder (FAOD) affecting 1 to 2 individuals per 100,000.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of VLCAD deficiency is established in a proband with a specific pattern of abnormal acylcarnitine levels on biochemical testing and/or by identification of biallelic pathogenic variants in ACADVL by molecular genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: ACADVL / very long chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: ACADVL | HGNC:92 | very long chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency | MONDO:0008723 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Frequent feeding and fasting limitation to prevent catabolic stress is the cornerstone of VLCAD management. Maximum fasting duration is age-dependent and shorter than in healthy individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Agents/circumstances to avoid: Fasting

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Dietary management consists of preventing periods of fasting and restricting fat intake by increasing carbohydrate intake, while maintaining an adequate and uninterrupted caloric intake.

nodes.description: Arrhythmias including ventricular fibrillation may occur in the severe neonatal form and are related to lipotoxic effects of accumulated long-chain acylcarnitines on cardiac conduction.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The severe early-onset cardiac and multiorgan failure form typically presents in the first months of life with hypertrophic or dilated cardiomyopathy, pericardial effusion, and arrhythmias

nodes.description: Dilated or hypertrophic cardiomyopathy predominantly in severe neonatal form. May present with pericardial effusion and arrhythmias.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The severe early-onset cardiac and multiorgan failure form typically presents in the first months of life with hypertrophic or dilated cardiomyopathy

nodes.description: Elevated serum CK during rhabdomyolysis episodes. In an adult cohort, mean CK was 68,958 U/L; pediatric cases report peaks up to 76,656 U/L.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of fatty-acid oxidation disorders in adults: Optimism despite the limited effective therapies available.
evidence.snippet: Episodes of rhabdomyolysis were frequent (84%), with a mean creatinine kinase level of 68,958 U/L

nodes.description: Rapid carbohydrate support during illness or decompensation via intravenous glucose (at least 10% dextrose) to reverse catabolism and prevent further lipolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Acute inpatient treatment: Administration of high-energy fluids (≥10% IV dextrose)

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet: The main measure in emergency hospital treatment is the administration of IV glucose.

nodes.description: Reduced exercise capacity and exercise-induced muscle symptoms. In adult cohorts, exercise intolerance is a principal symptom.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of fatty-acid oxidation disorders in adults: Optimism despite the limited effective therapies available.
evidence.snippet: The principal symptoms were acute muscle manifestations (rhabdomyolysis, exercise intolerance, myalgia), sometimes associated with permanent muscle weakness.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The later-onset episodic myopathic form presents with intermittent rhabdomyolysis provoked by exercise, muscle cramps and/or pain, and/or exercise intolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Low Fasting Concentrations of Glucagon in Patients with Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The glucagon and insulin levels were significantly lower in the VLCAD group compared to the CUD group

evidence.reference_title: Low Fasting Concentrations of Glucagon in Patients with Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Low fasting concentrations of glucagon are present in patients with VLCAD and cannot be explained by altered stimuli in plasma.

readouts.interpretation: Lower fasting glucagon is associated with VLCAD-related FAO impairment and may reflect reduced counter-regulatory support during fasting.
readouts.target: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

evidence.reference_title: Odd- and even-numbered medium-chained fatty acids protect against glutathione depletion in very long-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: metabolic stress led to GSH depletion and decreased viability in VLCAD deficient cells

readouts.interpretation: Glutathione is best interpreted as a stress-context-dependent readout: VLCAD-deficient cells can deplete GSH under metabolic stress even when broader metabolomic profiles suggest oxidative-stress remodeling.

nodes.description: Liver enlargement during acute metabolic decompensation, associated with hepatic steatosis from impaired fatty acid oxidation.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The hepatic or hypoketotic hypoglycemic form typically presents during early childhood with hypoketotic hypoglycemia and hepatomegaly

nodes.description: Secondary hyperammonemia during metabolic decompensation, resulting from impaired hepatic FAO support for ureagenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Early childhood disease may manifest with hypoketotic hypoglycemia, hyperammonemia, lactic acidosis, and elevated transaminases.

nodes.description: Hypoglycemia with inadequate ketone production during fasting or catabolic stress. A feared complication across all VLCAD subtypes.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The hepatic or hypoketotic hypoglycemic form typically presents during early childhood with hypoketotic hypoglycemia and hepatomegaly, but without cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Low Fasting Concentrations of Glucagon in Patients with Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Hypoketotic hypoglycemia is a feared clinical complication and the treatment focuses on avoiding hypoglycemia.

nodes.description: VLCAD-deficient cells show impaired TLR4 signaling and reduced pro-inflammatory cytokine induction after LPS stimulation, connecting long-chain FAO to innate immune competence and helping explain why infection and inflammation trigger metabolic decompensation.

evidence.reference_title: Human inborn errors of long-chain fatty acid oxidation show impaired inflammatory responses to TLR4-ligand LPS.
evidence.snippet: The insufficiencies included reduced TLR4 expression levels, impaired TLR4 signaling, and reduced or absent induction of pro-inflammatory cytokines such as IL-6.

nodes.description: Impaired mitochondrial oxidation of long-chain fatty acyl-CoA substrates (C12-C20), especially during fasting and other catabolic states, reduces acetyl-CoA generation, limits ATP production, and impairs hepatic ketogenesis.

biological_processes: very long-chain fatty acid metabolic process

evidence.reference_title: The Pathogenesis of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Within the mitochondria, fatty acid β-oxidation (FAO) and oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) are crucial metabolic processes involved in generating ATP, with defects in these pathways causing mitochondrial disease.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The hepatic or hypoketotic hypoglycemic form typically presents during early childhood with hypoketotic hypoglycemia and hepatomegaly, but without cardiomyopathy.

readouts.interpretation: Inappropriately low ketone bodies report impaired hepatic ketogenesis downstream of the long-chain FAO block.
Low ketone production is the biochemical readout of the hypoketotic component of VLCAD-associated hypoglycemia.

nodes.description: Elevated lactate during metabolic crises, reflecting impaired mitochondrial energy metabolism and compensatory anaerobic glycolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Early childhood disease may manifest with hypoketotic hypoglycemia, hyperammonemia, lactic acidosis, and elevated transaminases.

nodes.description: Low energy and reduced responsiveness during metabolic crises, associated with hypoglycemia and energy failure.

evidence.reference_title: Very long-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: the effects of accidental fat loading in a patient detected through newborn screening.
evidence.snippet: was admitted with emesis, severe lethargy, limpness in extremities, loss of muscle tone and an elevated CK level.

nodes.description: Accumulating long-chain acylcarnitines (especially C14:1 and related C14-C18 species) and fatty acid intermediates may contribute directly to pathology through increased cellular permeability, inflammation, calcium signaling disruption, and arrhythmogenic potential.

evidence.reference_title: A Distinctive Metabolomics Profile and Potential Biomarkers for Very Long Acylcarnitine Dehydrogenase Deficiency (VLCADD) Diagnosis in Newborns.
evidence.snippet: Two hundred and six significantly dysregulated endogenous metabolites were identified in VLCADD, in contrast to healthy newborns.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated long-chain acylcarnitine species report accumulation of incompletely oxidized long-chain FAO intermediates.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Urine organic acids are notable for extremely reduced or absent ketones, with elevated long-chain carboxylic and dicarboxylic acids.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated urinary long-chain carboxylic and dicarboxylic acids report abnormal long-chain substrate handling during VLCAD decompensation.

nodes.description: Dietary restriction of long-chain triglycerides to approximately 10% of total energy intake, while ensuring adequate essential fatty acid supply and monitoring fat-soluble vitamins.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet: In long-chain deficits, long-chain triglyceride restriction should be 10% of total energy, with linoleic acid and linolenic acid intake of 3-4% and 0.5-1%

evidence.reference_title: Management and Outcomes of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency (VLCAD Deficiency): A Retrospective Chart Review.
evidence.snippet: Patients who had a high C14:1 value at diagnosis were started on a diet with a lower percentage of energy from long-chain triglycerides.

nodes.description: MCT supplementation provides an alternative energy substrate that bypasses the VLCAD enzyme block. In long-chain FAODs, MCT is supplemented at 10-25% of total energy, with total MCT plus LCT maintained at 20-35% of energy.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet: In long-chain deficits, long-chain triglyceride restriction should be 10% of total energy, with linoleic acid and linolenic acid intake of 3-4% and 0.5-1% (5/1-10/1 ratio), with medium-chain triglyceride supplementation at 10-25% of total energy

nodes.description: Permanent proximal muscle weakness may develop in some patients, sometimes associated with the myopathic form.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of fatty-acid oxidation disorders in adults: Optimism despite the limited effective therapies available.
evidence.snippet: The principal symptoms were acute muscle manifestations (rhabdomyolysis, exercise intolerance, myalgia), sometimes associated with permanent muscle weakness.

nodes.description: Generalized hypotonia, particularly in the severe neonatal form, reflecting systemic energy deficit in skeletal muscle.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The severe early-onset cardiac and multiorgan failure form typically presents in the first months of life with hypertrophic or dilated cardiomyopathy, pericardial effusion, and arrhythmias, as well as hypotonia, hepatomegaly, and intermittent hypoglycemia.

nodes.description: Muscle pain is a common symptom, particularly in the later-onset myopathic form, and may occur independently of rhabdomyolysis episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of fatty-acid oxidation disorders in adults: Optimism despite the limited effective therapies available.
evidence.snippet: The principal symptoms were acute muscle manifestations (rhabdomyolysis, exercise intolerance, myalgia), sometimes associated with permanent muscle weakness.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The later-onset episodic myopathic form presents with intermittent rhabdomyolysis provoked by exercise, muscle cramps and/or pain, and/or exercise intolerance.

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in ACADVL cause VLCAD deficiency. The mutation spectrum includes missense, nonsense, splice-site, and small insertions/deletions. Genotype-phenotype correlations are imperfect; functional testing of residual enzyme activity provides better prognostic information than genotype alone.

evidence.reference_title: Management and Outcomes of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency (VLCAD Deficiency): A Retrospective Chart Review.
evidence.snippet: The C14:1 result at diagnosis is the value that has been guiding our initial clinical management in asymptomatic diagnosed newborns.

nodes.description: VLCAD deficiency increases reactive oxygen species production and renders cells vulnerable to glutathione depletion under metabolic stress. Mitochondrial NADPH production is relevant to GSH recycling and cellular resilience.

evidence.reference_title: A Distinctive Metabolomics Profile and Potential Biomarkers for Very Long Acylcarnitine Dehydrogenase Deficiency (VLCADD) Diagnosis in Newborns.
evidence.snippet: Two hundred and six significantly dysregulated endogenous metabolites were identified in VLCADD, in contrast to healthy newborns.

nodes.description: Patients at risk of rhabdomyolysis should ingest MCT or carbohydrates or a combination of both approximately 20 minutes before exercise to provide alternative energy substrates and reduce reliance on impaired long-chain FAO.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Patients at risk of rhabdomyolysis should ingest MCT or carbohydrates or a combination of both 20 min before exercise.

nodes.description: Recurrent muscle breakdown, often triggered by exercise, fasting, cold, or illness. In an adult FAOD cohort, rhabdomyolysis occurred in 84% of patients, with mean CK 68,958 U/L. VLCAD subgroup had 4.9 episodes per year pre-diagnosis, declining to 2.5 per year post-diagnosis.

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The later-onset episodic myopathic form presents with intermittent rhabdomyolysis provoked by exercise

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of fatty-acid oxidation disorders in adults: Optimism despite the limited effective therapies available.
evidence.snippet: Episodes of rhabdomyolysis were frequent (84%), with a mean creatinine kinase level of 68,958 U/L

nodes.description: VLCAD interacts physically with OXPHOS supercomplexes; VLCAD deficiency disrupts these interactions and impairs electron transport chain function, worsening bioenergetics beyond the primary FAO defect.
nodes.id: Secondary mitochondrial dysfunction and OXPHOS impairment

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, NADH to ubiquinone

evidence.reference_title: The Pathogenesis of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: there is growing evidence that other aspects of mitochondrial function are also affected in VLCADD, including secondary defects in OXPHOS function.

evidence.reference_title: The Pathogenesis of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: we describe what is now known about the protein-protein interactions between VLCAD and the OXPHOS supercomplex and how their disruption contributes to overall VLCADD pathogenesis.

nodes.description: Seizures, including status epilepticus or epileptic spasms, can occur as severe neurologic presentations in VLCAD deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Super-Refractory Status Epilepticus Progressing to Infantile Epileptic Spasms Syndrome Secondary to Very Long Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Super-Refractory Status Epilepticus Progressing to Infantile Epileptic Spasms Syndrome Secondary to Very Long Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Plasma acylcarnitine profile shows characteristic elevation of C14:1, C14:2, C14, and C12:1 species.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated C14:1 acylcarnitine reports the blocked long-chain FAO step in VLCAD deficiency.
Higher newborn-screening C14:1 predicts lower residual VLCAD enzyme activity, linking the biomarker to molecular-function deficiency.

nodes.description: Energy deficiency affects myocardium, skeletal muscle, and liver. Impaired FAO reduces availability of reducing equivalents and acetyl-CoA for mitochondrial ATP production and hepatic ketogenesis, forcing compensatory reliance on glucose utilization and gluconeogenesis.
nodes.id: Tissue energy deficit in high-demand organs

evidence.reference_title: Very Long-Chain Acyl-Coenzyme A Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The severe early-onset cardiac and multiorgan failure form typically presents in the first months of life with hypertrophic or dilated cardiomyopathy, pericardial effusion, and arrhythmias, as well as hypotonia, hepatomegaly, and intermittent hypoglycemia.

evidence.reference_title: Heptanoate Improves Compensatory Mechanism of Glucose Homeostasis in Mitochondrial Long-Chain Fatty Acid Oxidation Defect.
evidence.snippet: Defects in mitochondrial fatty acid β-oxidation (FAO) impair metabolic flexibility, which is an essential process for energy homeostasis.

nodes.description: FDA-approved odd-chain triglyceride therapy for long-chain FAODs. Triheptanoin provides anaplerotic substrates (propionyl-CoA) that support the TCA cycle and gluconeogenesis, dosed at approximately 20-35% of total energy intake. GI adverse effects may improve with dose fractionation.

evidence.reference_title: Heptanoate Improves Compensatory Mechanism of Glucose Homeostasis in Mitochondrial Long-Chain Fatty Acid Oxidation Defect.
evidence.snippet: Heptanoate is a suitable substrate to induce glucose production in mitochondrial FAO defect.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Trihepatnoin is a new therapeutic option with a good safety and efficacy profile.

evidence.reference_title: Management and Outcomes of Very Long-Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency (VLCAD Deficiency): A Retrospective Chart Review.
evidence.snippet: The study included 12 patients, 7 of whom had an enzyme activity of more than 10%, and 5 patients had an enzyme activity of less than 10%.

readouts.interpretation: Reduced measured VLCAD enzyme activity reports loss of ACADVL molecular function and helps stratify residual activity.

meta.term_label: Very Long-Chain Specific Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase, Mitochondrial

nodes.description: Vomiting associated with fasting intolerance and metabolic decompensation episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Very long-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: the effects of accidental fat loading in a patient detected through newborn screening.
evidence.snippet: was admitted with emesis, severe lethargy, limpness in extremities, loss of muscle tone and an elevated CK level.
